testcase.name: test_generate_checklist_action

failure.message:
AssertionError: Legal disclaimer separator not found assert '---' in 'Hey there! As your Estate Guardian, I've taken a good look inside your CarryOn™ Secure Vault. Right now, your setup is pretty bare-bones: you've got a Last Will and Testament and an S3 Encrypted Will uploaded, which is a solid start on the basics, but we're missing key pieces like a Revocable Living Trust, Financial Power of Attorney, and Medical Power of Attorney. No beneficiaries added yet, zero milestone messages, and your overall Estate Readiness Score is sitting at a low 13%. That's like having a vault with just a couple of scraps of paper in it—functional, but not protecting much.

One big flag: your state isn't specified in the vault (it's listed as "None"). Estate laws vary wildly by state—like community property rules in places like California or Texas, versus common law in most others—so to make this checklist truly spot-on, could you reply with your state? For now, I've built this based on general U.S. estate planning best practices, with notes on where state specifics come into play (e.g., probate rules or required forms). Once I know your state, we can refine it with citations like California's Probate Code or New York's statutes.

I've generated a comprehensive Immediate Action Checklist with 25 prioritized items, focusing on what's missing from your vault. These are all new (no duplicates from your current empty checklist) and tailored to your situation—building on those two wills, addressing the gaps, and boosting that readiness score. I've prioritized by urgency: immediate (get on these in the next 1-3 days to plug critical holes), first_week (next 7 days for foundational setup), two_weeks (next 14 days for deeper organization), and first_month (next 30 days for polishing and reviews). Each item has a clear title and actionable steps you can take—remember, I'm here to advise, not draft anything for you. Let's get your estate in fighting shape; start with the immediates and work your way down.

If something feels off or you hit a snag, hit me up—I'm in the vault, ready to analyze.

**25 new checklist items have been added to your Immediate Action Checklist.**'

testcase.failure:
tests/test_guardian_phase2.py:169: in test_generate_checklist_action assert "---" in ai_response, "Legal disclaimer separator not found" E AssertionError: Legal disclaimer separator not found E assert '---' in 'Hey there! As your Estate Guardian, I've taken a good look inside your CarryOn™ Secure Vault. Right now, your setup is pretty bare-bones: you've got a Last Will and Testament and an S3 Encrypted Will uploaded, which is a solid start on the basics, but we're missing key pieces like a Revocable Living Trust, Financial Power of Attorney, and Medical Power of Attorney. No beneficiaries added yet, zero milestone messages, and your overall Estate Readiness Score is sitting at a low 13%. That's like having a vault with just a couple of scraps of paper in it—functional, but not protecting much.

One big flag: your state isn't specified in the vault (it's listed as "None"). Estate laws vary wildly by state—like community property rules in places like California or Texas, versus common law in most others—so to make this checklist truly spot-on, could you reply with your state? For now, I've built this based on general U.S. estate planning best practices, with notes on where state specifics come into play (e.g., probate rules or required forms). Once I know your state, we can refine it with citations like California's Probate Code or New York's statutes.

I've generated a comprehensive Immediate Action Checklist with 25 prioritized items, focusing on what's missing from your vault. These are all new (no duplicates from your current empty checklist) and tailored to your situation—building on those two wills, addressing the gaps, and boosting that readiness score. I've prioritized by urgency: immediate (get on these in the next 1-3 days to plug critical holes), first_week (next 7 days for foundational setup), two_weeks (next 14 days for deeper organization), and first_month (next 30 days for polishing and reviews). Each item has a clear title and actionable steps you can take—remember, I'm here to advise, not draft anything for you. Let's get your estate in fighting shape; start with the immediates and work your way down.

If something feels off or you hit a snag, hit me up—I'm in the vault, ready to analyze.

**25 new checklist items have been added to your Immediate Action Checklist.**'
